entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Choosing the Ideal SAP Warehousing Solution for Your Company
entry.content:
In today's dynamic supply chain landscape, businesses require efficient and flexible warehouse management systems (WMS) to stay competitive. SAP offers robust solutions for optimizing warehouse operations. However, with multiple options available, such as Basic EWM, Advanced EWM, Decentralized EWM, and newer alternatives like SAP Stock Room Management and SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud, choosing the right one can be challenging.

This blog will explore each of these options and provide guidance on selecting the most suitable solution for your business.

Understanding the Different SAP Warehouse Management Options

1. SAP Stock Room Management

  • What It Is: Stock Room Management is a simplified warehouse management solution introduced within SAP S/4HANA. It offers core warehouse functionalities tailored for businesses with straightforward warehouse needs. SAP created Stock Room Management for customers migrating from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system to S/4HANA, but who do not require the advanced features of SAP EWM.
  • Key Features:
    • Core warehouse processes like stock transfers, goods receipt, and goods issue.
    • Basic inventory management functionalities.
    • Material handling, cycle counting, and internal movements.
    • Simplified user experience with integration into SAP S/4HANA.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses that require basic warehouse functionalities without the need for advanced automation or complex workflows. It is ideal for smaller warehouses or companies transitioning from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management system who want to stay within the SAP S/4HANA ecosystem without upgrading to full EWM.

2. SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • What It Is: SAP Warehouse Management (WM) on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-based warehouse management functionalities for businesses that want to move their operations to the cloud. It offers core WM features while leveraging the scalability and flexibility of the public cloud infrastructure.
  • Key Features:
    • Cloud-based deployment with access to core warehouse functionalities.
    • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • Real-time inventory visibility and warehouse operations.
    • Regular updates from SAP without the need for heavy customizations.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses looking for a scalable, cloud-based solution to manage basic warehouse operations, especially those that want to reduce infrastructure costs and simplify system management. It is an excellent option for companies transitioning to the cloud or that want a more agile and scalable warehouse management solution.

3. Basic EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Basic EWM is the foundational version of SAP EWM, embedded within SAP S/4HANA. It provides essential warehouse management features with no additional licensing costs.
  • Key Features:
    • Basic warehouse processes such as picking, packing, and goods receipt/goods issue.
    • Inventory management and stock transfers.
    • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for seamless data flow.
  • Best Suited For: Small to medium-sized businesses or warehouses with simple processes that don’t require advanced features like automation or complex workflows.

4. Advanced EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Advanced EWM is the more feature-rich version of SAP EWM, offering a comprehensive set of advanced warehouse management functionalities within SAP S/4HANA.
  • Key Features:
    • Complex warehouse operations, including cross-docking, wave management, slotting, and labor management.
    • Full support for automation systems (AGVs, conveyors).
    • Advanced stock management, including batch management and serialization.
    • Yard management, kitting, and value-added services.
  • Best Suited For: Medium to large businesses with complex warehouse processes that require high levels of automation and optimization.

5. Decentralized EWM

  • What It Is: Decentralized EWM is a stand-alone version of SAP EWM that operates on a separate server from the SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system, offering greater flexibility and scalability.
  • Key Features:
    • Runs independently from the SAP ERP system for improved flexibility.
    • Comprehensive functionality similar to Advanced EWM.
    • Designed for large warehouses with high transaction volumes and complex operations.
    • Multi-warehouse support and real-time process control.
  • Best Suited For: Large, global enterprises with multiple warehouses, complex operations, and high transaction volumes, where independence from the ERP system is crucial for uninterrupted operations.

Key Factors to Consider When Choosing the Right SAP Warehouse Management Solution

1. Warehouse Complexity

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are designed for businesses with straightforward warehouse operations. They are ideal if your processes are mostly manual, and you don’t require complex workflows or advanced automation.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer more comprehensive features, such as integration with automation, real-time process monitoring, and optimization. These are best for businesses with intricate warehouse setups, multiple locations, or advanced inventory management needs.
  • SAP WM on S/4HANA Public Cloud is suitable for companies seeking a cloud-first approach, providing core functionalities but with scalability and real-time access to inventory data.

2. Automation Needs

  • If your warehouse relies on automation or is planning to integrate automated systems (e.g., conveyors, AGVs, robots), you will need the capabilities of Advanced EWM or Decentralized EWM. These solutions offer robust support for integrating automation with your warehouse processes.
  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are better suited for manual operations or warehouses with low levels of automation.

3. Cloud Adoption Strategy

  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud is the optimal choice if your business is looking to adopt a cloud-based WMS. It provides flexibility, regular updates, and reduced infrastructure costs, making it ideal for companies focused on digital transformation and scalability.
  • For businesses that prefer an on-premise system or already have SAP S/4HANA in place, Embedded EWM (Basic or Advanced) or Decentralized EWM are viable options.

4. Cost and Licensing

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are included with SAP S/4HANA, making them cost-effective solutions for businesses that don’t need the additional features provided by Advanced or Decentralized EWM.
  • Advanced EWM requires additional licensing, but it offers significantly more functionality for businesses with complex warehouse needs.
  • Decentralized EWM comes with higher costs, as it requires separate infrastructure and licensing, but it is essential for large enterprises that need a fully independent warehouse management system.

5. Future Growth and Scalability

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are suitable for businesses with relatively stable warehouse operations that don’t anticipate significant changes in the near future.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer scalability, making them ideal for growing businesses or enterprises with plans to expand their warehouse operations, implement automation, or add new distribution centers.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-native scalability, making it an excellent choice for companies looking to grow their operations quickly and cost-effectively while leveraging cloud infrastructure.

Conclusion: Which SAP Warehouse Management Solution is Right for You?

Choosing the right SAP warehouse management solution depends on your business’s operational complexity, growth plans, and technological direction. Here's a summary of when each solution may be the best fit:

  • Stock Room Management: Best for businesses with simple warehouse operations that are moving from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system and want to continue with basic functionality within SAP S/4HANA.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud: Ideal for companies focused on adopting cloud solutions with core warehouse functionality and scalability.
  • Basic EWM: Perfect for small to medium-sized businesses that need essential warehouse management features as part of their SAP S/4HANA system without additional costs.
  • Advanced EWM: Suited for medium to large businesses with more complex warehouse operations, requiring advanced automation, inventory management, and process optimization.
  • Decentralized EWM: The best choice for large, multi-warehouse businesses that need a fully independent WMS, capable of handling high volumes and offering real-time, 24/7 operations.

By assessing your current and future warehouse needs, transaction volumes, levels of automation, and growth strategy, you can make an informed decision and choose the right SAP warehouse management solution to drive operational efficiency and support your business objectives.

entry.title: From CAD to Manufacturing as-a-Service. New Idea-to-Market Public Cloud Scenario available
entry.content:
Overview

The journey started in the mid-1990s with SAP R/3 and the CPI-C based CAD Interfaces, then over to iPPE, CAD Desktop, SAP PLM 7, SAP ECTR and S/4HANA and now to the new SAP PLM – SAP has constantly been supporting customers in their product development and market introduction efforts. Since the first releases we not only focused on typical PLM topics like CAD data management but also always provided an integrated path into the rest of the enterprise like purchasing or manufacturing.

Software for the Public Cloud now allows additional customer groups like growing enterprises, subsidiaries, country organizations or individual departments to benefit, … especially since we recently introduced a missing key capability in the public cloud-based SAP PLM - the new ‘Product Data Integration’ Software-as-a-Service application for public cloud-based CAD Integration. Together with this CAD Integration, we also provided all necessary enhancements to allow a smooth handover of product structures, routings, and products to SAP S/4HANA Cloud supporting downstream processes like purchasing and manufacturing.

What’s the benefit?

For the first time SAP is now offering a completely public cloud-based suite to support all processes relevant from early product definition and development through to manufacturing. As with all public cloud services, the effort that you need for implementing the software and keeping it up to date is reduced dramatically. In addition, you directly receive all the new features and developments – and not just years later. Several extensibility tools continue to allow you to enhance the software, always following the clean core guidance. Altogether, this makes it easier, especially for smaller units, to get started with software support for your product development processes.

Idea-to-Market

To lower the entry barrier for the implementation of SAP PLM, this all should be as simple as possible – especially since this additional deployment option mainly targets smaller units and smaller implementation partners. To provide additional guidance for the implementation, we now deliver process blueprints on SAP’s Business Accelerator Hub. On the Business Accelerator Hub, you can find reference content for all areas in an enterprise: https://api.sap.com/allprocess , documented by us following the same methodology. You’ll find all information for product development under ‘Idea to Market’. The new solution variant ‘Standard Engineering (Discreet Industries) for Cloud Deployment’ is now available here: https://api.sap.com/package/StandardEngineeringDiscreteIndustriesforPublicCloudDeployment/businessprocess. The four categories of diagrams we provide help you to understand with typical enterprise architecture views which business process scope is covered, which components are involved, how is the data flowing through the integrated system landscape, and more:

  • The value flow diagram depicts all business activities supported by the variant
  • The component diagram depicts all solution components needed to implement the business process
  • The data flow diagram depicts key flows of master and transactional data of the variant
  • The process flow diagram, based on the BPMN standard, depicts the process flow and used to specify the collaboration of the different solution components in more detail

From the big picture down to the more detailed process flow diagrams, you as our partners and customers can use this as a template for your implementations.

Several aspects like project management, prototyping, time confirmation will be picked up in future releases. Also engineer-to-order or configure-to-order will be covered in detail later.

What is covered by Idea-to-Market?

The described process starts with an idea for a new product or an improvement resulting from feedback or an ideation campaign. Sometimes you get several similar ideas. Therefore, several ideas can be aggregated into single needs. The ideas and needs are then linked to requirements. With the requirements structuring, a product manager has a clear overview as a basis for a transparent product planning process. From there (mostly for more complex products) either a systems model or a high-level product structure can be directly created. Additionally, CAD engineers can start working on the detailed design and with this also defining the bill-of-material.

Of course, there can be many iterations until the best design is available – these iterations can be managed with SAP PLM’s versioning concept of products and product structures. The built-in visualization service, configuration management, and collaboration features additionally support the development process. Once a certain maturity level has been reached the handover to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can be started. Only then the missing material masters, routings and bills-of-materials are created in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system for further processing.

In the next weeks we will also roll out this reference flow to SAP Cloud ALM and the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

Summary

SAP offers simplified access to idea-to-market support in the public cloud, especially for smaller or growing companies or units. Availability of detailed process templates helps our customers and partners reduce project risk and accelerate the project implementation. With the new ‘Idea-to-Market: Standard Engineering (Discrete Industries) for Public Cloud Deployment’, you now have a template process description available. Over time, additional content and variants (ETO, CTO or process industries) will be added as well.

Links:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub: Business Processes: https://api.sap.com/allprocess
  • Idea-to-Market: https://api.sap.com/process/ideaToMarket
  • Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/I2M?locale=en-US

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

There is a new commenting feature available for What’s New. Authors can now evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Outcome of the Q3/2024 Review
entry.content:
Dear Customers and Partners, 

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release, we would like to provide an update on the progress made regarding your requests submitted through the Customer Influence portal for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
In our ongoing commitment to improving our product with and for you, we have prioritized qualified requests while still giving due consideration to requests that did not meet the voting threshold, depending on strategic fit and capacity.
 
We are happy to announce that we have provided 270+ deliveries for your improvement requests since the last review cycle in June 2024. Please find below some highlights and progress made on other requests.

entry.title: Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation : Manage Credit Memo Request
entry.content:
Introduction....

SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) enables business users to automate workflow processes and tasks without writing code.

Check here for What's New and Learn more from the roadmap.  

This August 2024, SAP Build Process Automation has a released a new way of discovering Business Events from Lobby.

It enables users to discover events from Business Accelerator hub and to view the detailed event catalogue. It also facilitates reuse by creating centrally managed Events projects at Lobby level. Additionally using the Manual Event Specification Upload feature, all the SAP and non-SAP business events supporting CloudEvent1.0 format are supported which can be configured with Event Mesh(or Advanced Event Mesh) at runtime.

This blog will demonstrate the latest features of event triggers in the context of a SAP S/4HANA business use case:

  1. Monitoring Business Events tile
  2. Creating Event Projects from Lobby and consuming the events as event triggers in SAP Build Process Automation
  3. Run Now feature

The business use case explained….

Credit Memo Requests in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 

A credit memo request is a sales document used in complaints processing to request credit for a customer. Creating credit memo requests is a part of Sales and Distribution in SAP S/4HANA.  

In situations where the calculated prices are incorrect, or if discounts are not applied etc. a credit memo request can be created. The credit memo request can be automatically blocked for credit checking. Once it has been approved, you can remove the block. 

The system uses the credit memo request to create a credit memo. 

The SBPA Process for Manage Credit Memo Requests enables the business user to view the Credit Memo Request which are configured for external approvals and make a decision to Approve or Reject the Credit Memo Request which in turn will Release or Reject it in SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

 

Before we begin….

  • Credit Memo Requests are created in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 
  • Credit Memo Request lifecycle events are pushed from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to Business Technology Platform through the Event Mesh configuration. Follow the blog  Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation for details on the setup to be done.

 

Creating Event Projects From Lobby….

From the SAP Build Lobby, click on Events and click Create . 

 

There are two options - One to browse SAP Business Accelerator Hub to discover public business events or to upload an event specification file. Choose SAP Business Accelerator Hub for this particular use case.

Search with Credit Memo and Select Credit Memo Request Events SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Business Events

All the available Credit Memo Request Events are displayed with relevant details. Click Add.

The Project Name and Description is automatically populated. Click Create to create the event project.

The Event Project is now created. Click Release to release the project.

 

Choose the required option and click Release.

The Event Project is now released. Click Publish to publish the project to Library. Once the event project is released to Library it can be consumed in SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA).

Click Publish.

Now the Event Project is ready to be consumed in the SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA) process.

Adding the event trigger….

The events available in the events project released in the above step are available as a part of the Library and can be used in the SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA).

For example, when you add an Event Trigger to the process , it automatically shows the list of events available.

In the SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA) process, click on Add a Trigger.

Click on Wait for an Event.

Select CreditMemoRequest Created event and click on Add.

 

Note that all the required details are filled, click on Create.

 

The event trigger is added to the process.

 

Designing the process….

A sample business process for Manage Credit Memo Requests could look like the following:

 

 

  • An event trigger is used to start the business process. The event used here is CreditMemoRequestCreated Event.
  • A further API Call is made to read the header information of the Credit Memo Request. The Credit Memo Request ID from the previous step is used as input for this API Call. The API in this example is
  • GET /A_CreditMemoRequest('{CreditMemoRequest}')
  • A set of business rules define the approver for the Credit Memo Request approval. The output is an approver email ID.
  • The approver determined from the previous step gets an Approval Task to release the Credit Memo Request in My Inbox with details of header and items and an option to Approve or Reject the request.
  • If the approver approves the credit memo request, quotes are added to the Credit Memo Request ID and the Credit Memo Request is released for further processing in the SAP S/4HANA system. The approval status will show ‘Released’ as the status of the Credit Memo Request.  An approval notification is sent to the stakeholders.
  • The API used in this example is POST /releaseApprovalRequest
  • If the approver rejects the credit memo request, quotes are added to the Credit Memo Request ID and the Credit Memo Request Approval Status is updated as Rejected in SAP S/4HANA system. A rejection notification is sent to the stakeholders.
  • The API used in this example is POST /rejectApprovalRequest

Now our SBPA process is ready.

Expert Tip :  Ensure to test your Actions projects and Events setup before testing 
the process to ensure they are working as expected. 
This will save time for troubleshooting and debugging later.

 

End-to-end execution….

  • When a credit memo request is created in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and is configured for external approval, then it automatically triggers the defined process in SAP Build Process Automation.

 

  • Just for confirmation, check the Approval Status in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

 

  • Check if you received the event in Monitoring -> Acquired Events -> Business Events Tile. The events are persisted for 7 days and can be downloaded. Also they are very helpful in troubleshooting to understand if the events are correctly being received by SAP Build Process Automation.

 

 

  • You should get a task in My Inbox to Approve the Credit Memo Request

 

Add some comments and click approve.

When a Credit Memo Request is approved, it is released for further processing in the S/4HANA Public Cloud. The approval status will show ‘Released’ as the status of the Credit Memo Request   

 

OR  Add some comments and click Reject. Check that the S/4HANA Approval Status is updated as Rejected.

You can also check the SBPA Logs to check the details.

 

Run Now….

You can now test your processes locally that use event triggers by simulating the event trigger payload without connecting to the system or having an event mesh setup. This is to test if your end-to-end process works fine with the expected payload format for

“Run Now” action for event triggers in Trigger view allows to execute the event trigger in order to initiate event delivery with the user-provided data immediately. For more information, please refer to documentation here.

Below is  sample screenshot from Control Tower-> Environments -> Select your environment -> Triggers tab -> Select the required Event Trigger -> Run Now.

Enter a valid payload in the Run Now UI and Click Run Now.

 

The process is triggered with the sample payload.

 Note: The Manage Credit Memo Requests SBPA Process neither replaces nor has all the features of SAP Credit Management or Financial Supply Chain Management. It enables an approval process with information about the credit memo request. Our customers could use and extend this process for exception management, notifications, auditing for the particular credit memo requests after approval from one or more personas like credit manager, finance analysts, etc. 

Other use cases….

There are some additional use cases for business event triggers:

Business Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud : The Customer Return ...

Business Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA: Manage Credit Block on Sale...

Business Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA: Manage Billing Block on Sal...

Do write to me in case of blockers, queries and feedback.

entry.title: Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
With the 2408 release, Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition continues to grow to support new business models and more efficient ways of selling bundled solutions. Major milestones were reached in the previous 2402 release, with the inclusion of the much anticipated Advanced Solution Bundling capability, and the introduction of Sales Kits in solution orders. With 2408, this trajectory is continued with new enhancements which support greater flexibility when operating solution-based business models, enabling a seamless incorporation of solution orders into your business processes, and an improved user experience when creating and selling solution orders.  

Changeable Sales Orders in Solution Orders 


 One of the most significant updates with the 2408 release is the ability to create changeable sales orders from solution orders. If you're selling physical goods as part of a solution order, the process just got an important upgrade. Previously, once a sales order was generated from a solution order, it was in a read-only format, preventing any changes to be made post-release. Now, we have introduced the standard sales order type OR as the default when selling physical goods in a solution. This enables users to modify sales orders after they have been created, allowing for the addition of new products or changes to existing order details. 
 
This enhancement is expected to streamline the order management process and improve flexibility in handling customer orders, allowing sales teams the ability to adapt sales order items from a solution order post-release. Additionally, users can directly navigate to the edit view of the sales order from the solution order to make modifications. Changes to certain characteristics, such as confirmed delivery dates and quantities, are subsequently reflected in the user interface in solution order management. All changes made can be tracked in the change log of the sales item. Moreover, the pricing integration has been improved, offering greater flexibility in managing price copies through pricing types. A new pricing type has also been introduced for re-determining freight costs, discounts, and other costs within the sales order. 

 The benefits of modifiable sales orders extend beyond simple changes. The full range of sales order capabilities, such as advanced Available to Promise functionality, down payment processing, and advanced intercompany processing, is now enabled for goods sold as part of a solution order. Additionally, sales order type OR features which facilitate legal compliance are also now available in solution orders. In essence, all functions of standard sales order type OR processing are now available within solution order management. 

The flexibility brought about by using the standard sales order provides customers with the opportunity to re-define their business roles for solution orders to suit their needs. For example, it allows for the separation of order selling and order fulfillment, whereby a solution order specialist can create the solution order and track it’s progress, whilst a sales colleague can process order fulfilment, making changes to delivery attributes, for example. Watch the below video to see how this enhanced experience is designed to boost user productivity and streamline solution selling and fulfillment for customers. 
 
 

Non-valuated Products In Solution Orders 

In this release, we have grown the footprint of solution order management by introducing a powerful new feature for supporting short-term rental business models, enabling the inclusion of non-valuated products as part of a solution order. This allows businesses to deliver serialized physical products based on non-valuated items, which can be provided to customers without billing. Subsequently, the return of non-valuated products can be managed seamlessly via customer returns. 
 
To leverage this feature, businesses can use free-of-charge sales items without billing, categorized under item type SOR1. It’s essential to assign these products to item category group RP01 in the product master. The system processes these sales items with 100% discounted pricing, making them ideal for use as bundle subitems in solution orders. Upon releasing these items within a solution order, returnable items (item category RP01) are automatically created in follow-up sales orders (type OR only) for delivery, again without billing and with full discount pricing. 

Also supported is tracking of the rented goods in warehouse stock at the individual customer level, and to round off the process, the return of non valuated goods are at the end of the rental agreement is support via customer returns, also including returns with inspection where necessary. 

 
Sales Kits Type CPFH In Solution Orders

With the 2408 release, we extend the capabilities for bill of materials sales in solution orders by including the item category group CPFH as sales items within solution orders. This enhancement allows the system to process these sales BOMs at both the main item and subitem levels, enabling the application of a single, consolidated price to the entire BOM at the main item level.  

Updates to Data Migration Object

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, migration templates are predefined tools used to facilitate the migration of data from legacy systems to the cloud ERP system. These templates are part of the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit, which simplifies the process of transferring data into the cloud. Migration templates help ensure that the data is properly formatted and mapped to the correct fields in the target SAP system. Since the migration template for Solution Orders was introduced in the 2108 release, the functional scope of solution orders has grown considerably. Therefore, with the 2408 release, gaps in the migration template have been addressed and new capabilities added to reflect the current footprint of solution order management. With upgrades made in 2408, the migration template for Solution Orders now supports fields for linking existing projects, PCS pricing calculation for subscription items, and configurable products for sales order and service order and service contract items. 

 

Rateplan Selection for On Subscription Items

Subscription billing is not a native feature of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, however is integrated with solution order management to enable the sale of subscription products as part of a solution order. This means that SAP S/4HANA Cloud integrates with SB for managing subscription-related processes. A significant feature introduced in Subscription Billing which is now supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the ability to define multiple rate plans for a single subscription.  

A rate plan is essentially a set of pricing rules and conditions applied to a subscription. It determines how much a customer is charged and how frequently those charges occur. In Subscription Billing, rate plans include various parameters that govern not only the price but also billing cycles, discounts, and other financial elements. In Subscription Billing, the ability to define multiple rate plans for a single subscription has been introduced, and Solution Order Management has been enhanced to support this update for subscription items sold in a solution order. Previously, businesses had to create separate master data entries for each variation of a rate plan (e.g., different pricing models or billing cycles). This led to a bloated master data setup with multiple entries for what could essentially be one product or service. Now, with multiple rate plans, a single master data entry can support different pricing options, which simplifies the data management process. The user can select the most appropriate rate plan (e.g., monthly, yearly, or usage-based) at the time of creating the solution order, depending on the customer’s preference or contract terms. 
 
Supporting multiple rate plans on subscription items in solution order management has numerous benefits. By reducing the required master data with fewer entries to support different subscription configurations, business can manage data more efficiently. It enables greater flexibility in the solution order offering via different pricing models or billing cycles, and the enhancement streamlines order creation by enabling users to easily select from different rate plans directly in solution order management. Multiple rate plans will benefit customers looking to offer tiered pricing as part of their business models, whilst also supporting geographical variations in pricing and billing to cater to customers in different regions. 


  
Credit Memo and Debit Memo Requests for Invoices with Services and Subscriptions

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, invoicing for subscription billing and service transactions generated from a solution order have been enhanced with credit memos and debit memos. A credit memo request is a pre-approval document created to reduce the amount owed by a customer on a specific invoice. After approval, the system generates a credit memo, which reflects the agreed-upon reduction and adjusts the customer’s balance accordingly. On the other side, a debit memo request is created when the amount invoiced to the customer was lower than what was owed. It’s a request for additional charges or corrections, often issued when services were underbilled or additional services were provided. Once approved, a debit memo is generated to increase the customer’s balance by the additional amount. 

For solution orders that combine products, services, and subscriptions into a single order, credit and debit memo requests are handled at the solution order level to ensure consistency across products, services, and subscription billing. The system automatically links the credit or debit memo request to the original solution order, ensuring that adjustments are applied correctly. By supporting credit and debit memos, solution order invoicing is made more accurate and customer-friendly with clearer invoicing. 

 

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Sales, please see the Link Collection for Solution Order Management here

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn

 

 

️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Did you miss the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 50-minute-long recording and a presentation for Sales as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

 

Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Help! Why Don’t I Get a Notification for My Custom Situation?
entry.content:
You’ve configured your own situation use case using the Extended Framework for Situation Handling. Now you’re waiting for a notification but nothing happens. What could have gone wrong?

Let me ask you a few questions to check whether your configuration is correct.

  • Has a situation occurred?
  • How have the conditions been set up?
  • Are all situation triggers covered?
  • Is recipient determination working?
  • Have email notifications been set up correctly?

These questions can support you as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA customer.

 

Has a situation occurred?

This may seem trivial but depending on the conditions that have been defined for the situation type, a situation instance may not have been created. An easy way to find out is for the user is to check the My Situations – Extended app to see if there’s an instance that they should have been notified about. If the instance is not displayed in the app, continue checking the conditions. If there’s an instance but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle settings.

List of situaitons in My Situations – Extended app

Or, as a key user, you can display the current situation instances for a situation type by choosing Manage Instances in the header of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app.

Manage Instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

In the list of situation instances you can see if a notification was sent and when. Depending on the configuration, a notification can be sent only when a situation occurs for the first time or each time the situation is updated. If you don’t see an entry here, no notification was sent.

List of situation instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

There’s also the Monitor Situations – Extended app for key users that shows all created situation instances and all activities for an instance. You need to have situation monitoring enabled for the situation type in the Situation Instances section.

Enable the monitoring in Manage Situations - Extended app

List of situation instances in the Monitor Situations - Extended app

If no situation instance is displayed in the Manage Situation Types – Extended app and in the Monitor Situations – Extended app, this means that the users did not receive a notification because there no situation instance was created.  

If you expected a situation based on the conditions that you have defined, you may find clues in the following section on conditions.

If a situation instance is displayed but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle setting.

How have the conditions been set up?

In the Conditions section of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app, let’s look at the settings for Notifications / Life Cycle, the Order of the conditions, and the Filters.

Notifications / life cycle settings

Each condition can have a different Notifications/Life Cycle setting. Make sure you’ve assigned the appropriate ones.

Notification setting

You can select from three standard options:

  1. The notification is sent to all recipients when a situation is created. When the situation is updated, only the assigned processors are notified.
  2. No one receives a notification after a situation is closed.
  3. All recipients receive a notification after a closed situation has been reopened (this option is available only for object-based situations with the instance closing behavior Close & Keep)

If you’ve defined your own Notification/Life Cycle behavior, I recommend double-checking the life cycle transitions. Make sure you have considered all relevant transitions from creation to closure. And check the notification settings for each transition.

Consider the processing order of conditions

If you’ve set up multiple conditions, the order of those conditions is important. The order defines the sequence in which the conditions are executed. It’s important to understand that the processing of the conditions stops when the first condition in the processing order is met. Conditions lower in the sequence are ignored. For this reason, you need to ensure that the more specific conditions, for which you want to have notifications, are being processed before the more generic conditions. Pay special attention to conditions that do not send notifications and those that close instances. If they are too high up in the sequence other conditions may always be ignored.

Processing order of conditions

Condition filters

Effective filters need to be of the same data format as the values in the CDS fields, such as strings or integers. You can find the reference to your object’s CDS views in the Manage Situation Objects app.

Sometimes the conditions and their filter values have been set up in such a way that no instances are created. Check whether one of the following reasons has prevented the creation of a situation instance:

  • The condition attribute filters are too constrained for your use case.
  • The attribute filter values overlap.
  • The attribute filter values are contradictory.

The condition and filter settings depend on your use case and the situation template on which your situation type is based. If you use multiple conditions, you can easily compare filters using the Compare Filters function.

Compare condition filters

Compare condition filters used

You can find some examples of using multiple conditions with filters in the blog posts Custom Situation Cases: Configure an Escalation Case and Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

 

Are all situation triggers covered?

Complex use cases often require multiple situation triggers. Check whether you have configured all possible triggers. And ensure that all triggers are enabled. There are two kinds of triggers: batch-based and event-based triggers.

Batch-based triggers periodically check the data you have defined in the condition filters.

  • The triggering object can be the anchor object or the trigger object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object is always Add/Update because the object still exists.

Use the Trigger Batch function to test immediately whether the situation trigger works as expected. Also check batch scheduling to make sure it’s configured correctly.

Batch-based situation triggers

Event-based triggers are based on an object’s status update. You can use multiple event-based triggers where you need to consider three aspects:

  • The triggering object: Multiple objects may impact a situation.
  • The trigger events such as create, update, and remove: Multiple events may impact a situation for a specific object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object can be Add/Update, Remove (Trigger data available), or Remove (Trigger data not available)

Event-based situation triggers

And these three aspects need to go hand in hand.

Situation objects that are created always have the trigger object behavior Add/Update because the object is added and exists.

The events that change an object can be manifold, for example, updated, confirmed, rejected, in progress, for approval, completed, released, posted, published, started, activated, blocked, unlocked, and many more. If the object still exists after the change, the trigger object behavior is Add/Update. If the object is flagged as removed or deleted, the trigger object behavior is Remove (Trigger data available). 

Objects that are deleted always have the trigger object behavior Remove (trigger data not available). This means the object is physically gone along with all its data. No more references are possible.

You can find an example for multiple triggers in the blog post Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

Should a situation trigger be missing in the situation type, you have the following options in the test system:

  1. Add a situation trigger from the value help in the situation type.
  2. If no appropriate situation trigger is available for the situation type, you can add a situation trigger with the suitable event to your situation scenario (Manage Situation Scenarios app). To do this you need to have the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (1).  Optionally, you can add the situation trigger to the related template so that it’s automatically available for all situation types created from the situation template.
  3. If no matching situation trigger is available in the situation scenario, you can enhance or add a situation object with the missing situation trigger (Manage Situation Objects app). Again, you need the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (2) and (1).

Afterwards, transport your situation type, situation scenario, and situation object to the productive system.

 

Is recipient determination working?

Situation Handling uses Responsibility Management to determine the users that are responsible for a situation. In the My Situations – Extended app, users see the created situations, and, depending on the notification settings, receive notifications.

When you create a situation type in the Recipients section, there are two basic options for defining these users:

  • Responsibility by Teams, which determines the users with the help of a team category.
  • Responsibility Rules, which determines the users with a BAdI business logic.

Situation recipients

Responsibility by Teams

To determine the right users by teams, a team category needs to be in place. The team category links Situation Handling and Responsibility Management. For a team category, at least one team needs to be enabled so that the right users can be detected. You enable teams in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app. Also check whether all enabled teams have team members assigned to them and include the users who have to be informed about the situation.

Situation recipients by teams

You can add team members by selecting users from the value help. If you can’t find the user in the value help, check whether this user is registered as a business partner. If the business partner registration is missing, contact your system administrator to have your users registered.

If you use filters for responsibility definition or member functions in your situation type, make sure they are appropriate and don’t exclude your users. For detailed information about these filters, see the blog post Using Responsibility Teams in Situation Handling.

Responsibility Rules

Responsibility rules use the logic of a BAdI to determine the right users. If the identification of users isn’t working as expected, the logic of the BAdI can be the reason why your users were not found and therefore did not get any notifications.

Situation recipients by rules

If your situation type uses a standard responsibility rule, please open a ticket on component CA-GTF-RM to have the rule checked.

For your custom implementation, you can check the BAdI implementation logic yourself. (You’ll need the SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC role to do that). Then proceed as follows:

  • In the Manage Responsibility Context app, on the Extended tab, search for the parent responsibility context to find your extended responsibility context.
  • Open the extended responsibility context to display the details. Here, you can see the responsibility rule ID associated with the responsibility rule that is displayed in the situation type.
  • In the Custom Logic app, you need to filter the list for the responsibility rule business context. Then, search for the static filter value that is identical to the responsibility rule ID. In the details, you can then check the BAdI implementation logic.

For detailed information about custom responsibility rules, you can refer to the blog post Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules. The blog post Using Responsibility Rules in Situation Handling illustrates how responsibility rules and Situation Handling work hand in hand.

 

Have email notifications been set up correctly?

In addition to the SAP Fiori launchpad notifications, users can opt to receive email notifications for each situation type in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. For details, see Enable Email Notifications for End Users.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 email notifications are enabled automatically. In older releases users need to enable email notifications in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. To be able to activate this option, users need to have received an SAP Fiori launchpad notification before they can see and select the situation type in the launchpad settings.

Notification settings

Users may also want to check the launchpad settings to see whether the email address for their user account is correct:

Email settings

If all the settings are correct and you're still not getting any emails, you may want to check whether the email domain is on the list of allowed domains. The list of sender domains is maintained in Output Management. You can find more information in the chapter Defining an Email Sender Domain in the Setup Guide for SAP Best Practices for Output Management.

For specific questions or issues with the notification framework, please open a ticket on component OPU-GW-NOT.

 

Additional prerequisites for SAP S/4HANA

As an SAP S/4HANA customer, you may need to check some additional settings for the Notification Channel Framework which are automatically in place for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers. You can also refer to the blog post Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework. If you want to enable email notifications per default, you can implement the SAP Note 3417113.

To be on the safe side, you may also want to check whether you have made the required technical configuration settings.

  • Set up technical jobs
  • Activate OData services for apps
  • Define authorization for apps (optional)
  • Activate OData services for APIs (optional)
  • Define authorizations for APIs (optional)

 

Did this help you?

If not → just open a ticket on our component CA-SIT-ENG and we’ll take care of your problem. In the ticket, please let us know the situation scenario and the situation template your situation type is based on. Please attach screenshots of your situation type’s Conditions and Recipients sections. Or tell us which of the steps above you’ve already tried.

 

For more information, see

SAP Help Portal for

  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP Community

  • Intelligent Situation Handling

entry.title: Search, Select, and Straighten
entry.content:
Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) offers a wide variety of tools for managing configurable products. Characteristics and their values, describing the properties of a configurable material, make up the scaffold of configuration models – including a more or less complex net of dependencies between the characteristics and characteristic values.

Sometimes, the number of characteristics in a model can reach an enormous level, and you can quickly lose track of things: Which group does the characteristic belong to? Where in the BOM hierarchy is it located? It would be nice if you could search for characteristics in a vast model effectively, and even better if you could also directly valuate the characteristic you’ve been searching for. As you might have guessed, we’ve got good news: Searching and working within search results is now possible (since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408).

This is how it works: On the valuation screen in the simulation, there’s a new search field.

Here, you can enter the technical name or description of a characteristic. Whether you can use the technical name or the description (or both) depends on the settings for the description mode:

You can adjust this setting to your ease.

The search is case-insensitive and, important to know, it comprises all characteristic groups and configurable components of the bill of materials (BOM). This way, you’re not limited to the selected characteristic group on the screen.

As you can see in the following example, searching for "length" returns results from different BOM positions in the model:

You can immediately assign (or remove) values to the displayed characteristics when you see the results list.

This new feature will make handling the characteristic values of even humongous models much easier!

Check this link to our documentation to watch a short video showing the search feature: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/11d7452297a74a499d56f5049daa3606.html?version=2408.500.

entry.title: How to create custom cubes, queries and measurements using SAP key user tools
entry.content:
SAP key user tools can support end user to create their own cubes, queries and custom measurements. In this blog, we use sales analytics CDS View as example.

Needed Roles:

  • SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST(Create Customizing Queries)
  • SAP_BR_SALES_MANAGER(Show sales and billing analytics data)

Create Custom Cube

1. Open app "Custom CDS Views" and click "Create".

 2. Input view name and select the scenario Analytical Cube.

 3. Add the primary data source, e.g., I_BillingDocumentCube

 4. If primary data source cannot cover all required value, you can add associate data source. Click "Add" -> "Associated Data Source".

 5. Select associated data source.

 6. Click the button "Join Condition" to add join condition if required.

 

7. Add join condition. E.g., Billingdocument and billingdocumentitem 

 

8. You can define Parameter Value if parameter is required. After setting is done, check the color from red to blue. Go to tap Elements, click "Add"->"Elements".

 

9. Add all the elements what we need in the report.

 10. Go to tap "Elements Properties". During searching each fields, check the selections are correct.

 

11. Go to tap "Filter", if you need some filter options, e.g., sales organization.

 

12. All are done, click "Check" and "Preview data".

 

13. Click button "Publish". Custom CDS View is completed.

 14. Click tap "Next Steps" and choose button "Custom Analytical Queries".

 

Create Custom Query based on created custom cube

15. Click "New" to create new query. (App "Custom Analytical Queries"). Add Query Name and Data Source which is created in the last, then click "OK".

 

16. Filed selection(For example, Tax Amount, Net Value, Sakes Volume Qty, Credit Memos Qty, Sales Volume, Credit Memos, Billing Document etc.,)

Create Custom Measurements

 17. In section "Display", click "Add"->"Add Converted Measure". If the measurements only have transaction currencies, this function can convert the transaction currency to a fixed currency or be an external input.

 18. Input Label and Name. Select type "Currency Conversion". Then click "OK".

 19. Select value help of Target Currency.

 20. You can select a fixed value.

 21. You can also select User Input Filters.

 22. Add Calculated Measure. This function can enable you design your own measurement using existing fields/measurements.

 23. Input Expression. For example, Amount with Tax = "Net Value" - "Tax Amount", Net Quantity = "Sales Volume Qty" - "Credit Memos Qty".

 24. In section "Filters", set some filters on dimension if necessary. Preview the content of custom analytical query by click "Preview" button, and you can check the preview data if it is as expected.

 

25. Input exchange rate type and display currency, then click Go. This query will be previewed in Web Dynpro framework.

 

26. Click back, page will be back to your custom query. Save draft and click "Publish".

 27. To save the custom analytical report to your homepage of launchpad, click "Preview" and you can click "Save as Tile".

 

Save report as a Tile

28. Input Tile and select a Page. Then you can open this query from your launchpad.

 29. If you want to share the custom analytical report to others which means the report can be visible and accessed by authorized users. Open app "View Browser".

 30.Select the custom analytical query which you created, and click "Create Application". After the application is created, you can see the Application ID available to the custom analytical query.

 31. Enter Title and Subtitle, then click "OK".

 

32. Application is created.

 

33. Click the application ID  link, then a popup dialog will display. Click "View in Catalog" to navigate to app "Custom Catalog Extensions".

 

34. Add custom analytical query to business catalogs. Click "Add"

 

 35. Search the business catalog.

 36. Select the business catalogs which you want to  publish, click "Publish"

 37. When the status is published, the custom analytical report should be available to the users who have been assigned the business catalogs.

 

38. In app finder, the custom analytical report is displayed in assigned business catalogs. You can add it to pages.

 39. You can open your custom analytical report from your launchpad.

entry.title: SAP Product Inspiration Series for Industries & Intelligent CX (Wave #4)
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Today I'm more than excited to let you know we go full steam ahead with our Product Inspiration Video Series into the 2nd half of the year and covering the latest innovations of SAP Intelligent CX along with S/4HANA, Business AI, Joule, BTP and industry-tailored solutions. Please find the latest deep dive videos from our product teams below.

 

1. Mediafly AI Coaching & Enablement for SAP Sales Cloud & Service Cloud Version 2

Ben Turner, Director Product Management at Mediafly provides an overview on AI Coaching and Enablement for SAP Customer Experience. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction of Mediafly in building this solution with latest integrations to SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 as well as our overall innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.
